{{Infobox ancient site |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |other_names=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |group={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |shape=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |construction=Late 6th century–early 7th century |heritage_status=None |map2={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Sannōzuka Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=23|lat_sec=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been designated as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier forming a flat platform, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south. Artifacts recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. Based on its design and artifacts, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following dimensions: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves artifacts from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! Shape !! Length !! Construction Period !! || Early 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || 128m || Late 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Katatsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Scallop-shaped || 80m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Keyhole-shaped || 78.5m || Late 6th century || Prefectural Historic Site |- | {{Infobox ancient site |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |other_names=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |group={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |shape=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |construction=Late 6th century–early 7th century |heritage_status=None |map2={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Sannōzuka Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=23|lat_sec=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been designated as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier forming a flat platform, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south. Artifacts recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. Based on its design and artifacts, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following dimensions: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves artifacts from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! Shape !! Length !! Construction Period !! A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south. Artifacts recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. Based on its design and artifacts, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following dimensions: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves artifacts from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! {{Infobox ancient site |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |other_names=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |group={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |shape=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |construction=Late 6th century–early 7th century |heritage_status=None |map2={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Sannōzuka Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=23|lat_sec=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been designated as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier forming a flat platform, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south. Artifacts recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. Based on its design and artifacts, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following dimensions: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves artifacts from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! {{Infobox ancient site |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |other_names=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |group={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |shape=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |construction=Late 6th century–early 7th century |heritage_status=None |map2={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Sannōzuka Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=23|lat_sec=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been designated as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier forming a flat platform, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south. Artifacts recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. Based on its design and artifacts, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following dimensions: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves artifacts from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been designated as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier forming a flat platform, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south. Artifacts recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. Based on its design and artifacts, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following dimensions: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves artifacts from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! Shape !! Length !! Construction Period !! || Early 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || 128m || Late 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Katatsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塚古墳 revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |other_names=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |group={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |shape=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |construction=Late 6th century–early 7th century |heritage_status=None |map2={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Sannōzuka Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=23|lat_sec=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been designated as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier forming a flat platform, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south. Artifacts recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. Based on its design and artifacts, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following dimensions: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves artifacts from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! Shape !! Length !! Construction Period !! Heritage Status |- | {{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || 120m || Late 5th century || National Historic Site |- | [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]|| Keyhole-shaped || 123m || Early 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || 128m || Late 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Katatsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Scallop-shaped || 80m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Keyhole-shaped || 78.5m || Late 6th century || Prefectural Historic Site |- | '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || 89m || Late 6th century – Early 7th century || None |- | {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Circular || 65m || Early 7th century || Prefectural Historic Site |} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kokubun Kofun Group|title={{ill|日本歴史地名大系|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 9, Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Fujita, Norio|year=2002|chapter=Sannōzuka Kofun|title={{ill|続 日本古墳大辞典|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves artifacts from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! {{Infobox ancient site |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |other_names=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |group={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |shape=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |construction=Late 6th century–early 7th century |heritage_status=None |map2={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Sannōzuka Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=23|lat_sec=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been designated as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier forming a flat platform, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south. Artifacts recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. Based on its design and artifacts, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following dimensions: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves artifacts from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! Shape !! Length !! Construction Period !! {{Infobox ancient site |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |other_names=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |group={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |shape=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |construction=Late 6th century–early 7th century |heritage_status=None |map2={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Sannōzuka Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=23|lat_sec=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been designated as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier forming a flat platform, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. Artifacts recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. Based on its design and artifacts, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following dimensions: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves artifacts from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! Shape !! Length !! Construction Period !! || Early 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || 128m || Late 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Katatsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塚古墳 '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || 89m || Late 6th century – Early 7th century || None |- | {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Circular || 65m || Early 7th century || It opens to the south and has the following dimensions: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves artifacts from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |other_names=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |group={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |shape=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |construction=Late 6th century–early 7th century |heritage_status=None |map2={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Sannōzuka Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=23|lat_sec=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been designated as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier forming a flat platform, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. Artifacts recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. Based on its design and artifacts, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following dimensions: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves artifacts from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! Shape !! Length !! Construction Period !! Heritage Status |- | {{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || 120m || Late 5th century || National Historic Site |- | [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]|| Keyhole-shaped || 123m || Early 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || 128m || Late 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Katatsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Scallop-shaped || 80m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Keyhole-shaped || 78.5m || Late 6th century || Prefectural Historic Site |- | '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || 89m || Late 6th century – Early 7th century || None |- | {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Circular || 65m || Early 7th century || Prefectural Historic Site |} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kokubun Kofun Group|title={{ill|日本歴史地名大系|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 9, Tochigi revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |other_names=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |group={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |shape=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |construction=Late 6th century–early 7th century |heritage_status=None |map2={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Sannōzuka Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=23|lat_sec=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been designated as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier forming a flat platform, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. Artifacts recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. Based on its design and artifacts, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following dimensions: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves artifacts from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! Shape !! Length !! Construction Period !! Heritage Status |- | {{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || 120m || Late 5th century || National Historic Site |- | [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]|| Keyhole-shaped || 123m || Early 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || 128m || Late 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Katatsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Scallop-shaped || 80m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Keyhole-shaped || 78.5m || Late 6th century || Prefectural Historic Site |- | '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || 89m || Late 6th century – Early 7th century || None |- | {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Circular || 65m || Early 7th century || Prefectural Historic Site |} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kokubun Kofun Group|title={{ill|日本歴史地名大系|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 9, Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Fujita, Norio|year=2002|chapter=Sannōzuka Kofun|title={{ill|続 日本古墳大辞典|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum {{Infobox ancient site |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |other_names=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |group={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |shape=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |construction=Late 6th century–early 7th century |heritage_status=None |map2={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Sannōzuka Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=23|lat_sec=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been designated as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier forming a flat platform, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. Artifacts recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. Based on its design and artifacts, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following dimensions: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves artifacts from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! Shape !! Length !! Construction Period !! Heritage Status |- | {{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || 120m || Late 5th century || National Historic Site |- | [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]|| Keyhole-shaped || 123m || Early 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || 128m || Late 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Katatsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Scallop-shaped || 80m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Keyhole-shaped || 78.5m || Late 6th century || Prefectural Historic Site |- | '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || 89m || Late 6th century – Early 7th century || None |- | {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Circular || 65m || Early 7th century || Prefectural Historic Site |} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kokubun Kofun Group|title={{ill|日本歴史地名大系|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 9, Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Fujita, Norio|year=2002|chapter=Sannōzuka Kofun|title={{ill|続 日本古墳大辞典|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum It opens to the south and has the following dimensions: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves artifacts from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |other_names=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |group={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |shape=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |construction=Late 6th century–early 7th century |heritage_status=None |map2={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Sannōzuka Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=23|lat_sec=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been designated as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier forming a flat platform, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. Artifacts recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. Based on its design and artifacts, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following dimensions: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves artifacts from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! Shape !! Length !! Construction Period !! || Early 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || 128m || Late 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Katatsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Scallop-shaped || 80m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Keyhole-shaped || 78.5m || Late 6th century || Prefectural Historic Site |- | revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |other_names=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |group={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |construction=Late 6th century–early 7th century |heritage_status=None |map2={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Sannōzuka Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=23|lat_sec=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been designated as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier forming a flat platform, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. Artifacts recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. Based on its design and artifacts, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following dimensions: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves artifacts from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! Shape !! Length !! Construction Period !! Heritage Status |- | {{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || 120m || Late 5th century || National Historic Site |- | [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]|| Keyhole-shaped || 123m || Early 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || 128m || Late 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Katatsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Scallop-shaped || 80m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Keyhole-shaped || 78.5m || Late 6th century || Prefectural Historic Site |- | '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || 89m || Late 6th century – Early 7th century || None |- | {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Circular || 65m || Early 7th century || Prefectural Historic Site |} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kokubun Kofun Group|title={{ill|日本歴史地名大系|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 9, Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Fujita, Norio|year=2002|chapter=Sannōzuka Kofun|title={{ill|続 日本古墳大辞典|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |other_names=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |group={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |construction=Late 6th century–early 7th century |heritage_status=None |map2={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Sannōzuka Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=23|lat_sec=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been designated as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier forming a flat platform, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. Artifacts recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. Based on its design and artifacts, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following dimensions: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves artifacts from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! Shape !! Length !! Construction Period !! Heritage Status |- | {{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || 120m || Late 5th century || National Historic Site |- | [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]|| Keyhole-shaped || 123m || Early 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || 128m || Late 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Katatsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Scallop-shaped || 80m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Keyhole-shaped || 78.5m || Late 6th century || Prefectural Historic Site |- | '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || 89m || Late 6th century – Early 7th century || None |- | {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Circular || 65m || Early 7th century || Prefectural Historic Site |} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kokubun Kofun Group|title={{ill|日本歴史地名大系|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 9, Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Fujita, Norio|year=2002|chapter=Sannōzuka Kofun|title={{ill|続 日本古墳大辞典|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum {{Infobox ancient site |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |other_names=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |group={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |construction=Late 6th century–early 7th century |heritage_status=None |map2={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Sannōzuka Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=23|lat_sec=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been designated as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier forming a flat platform, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. Artifacts recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. Based on its design and artifacts, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following dimensions: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves artifacts from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! Shape !! Length !! Construction Period !! {{Infobox ancient site |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |other_names=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |group={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |construction=Late 6th century–early 7th century |heritage_status=None |map2={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Sannōzuka Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=23|lat_sec=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been designated as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier forming a flat platform, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. Artifacts recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. Based on its design and artifacts, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following dimensions: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves artifacts from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! {{Infobox ancient site |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |other_names=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |group={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |construction=Late 6th century–early 7th century |heritage_status=None |map2={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Sannōzuka Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=23|lat_sec=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been designated as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier forming a flat platform, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. Artifacts recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. Based on its design and artifacts, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following dimensions: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves artifacts from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! Shape !! Length !! Construction Period !! Heritage Status |- | {{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || 120m || Late 5th century || National Historic Site |- | [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]|| Keyhole-shaped || 123m || Early 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || 128m || Late 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Katatsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Scallop-shaped || 80m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Keyhole-shaped || 78.5m || Late 6th century || Prefectural Historic Site |- | '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || 89m || Late 6th century – Early 7th century || None |- | {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} || Circular || 65m || Early 7th century || Prefectural Historic Site |} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kokubun Kofun Group|title={{ill|日本歴史地名大系|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} Vol. 9, Tochigi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490093|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Fujita, Norio|year=2002|chapter=Sannōzuka Kofun|title={{ill|続 日本古墳大辞典|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum {{Infobox ancient site |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |other_names=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |group={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |construction=Late 6th century–early 7th century |heritage_status=None |map2={{location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Sannōzuka Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=23|lat_sec=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been designated as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier forming a flat platform, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. Artifacts recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. Based on its design and artifacts, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following dimensions: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves artifacts from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! || Early 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || 128m || Late 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Katatsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塚古墳
