Kofun (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |alternate_name=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |part_of={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} |burial goods=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |notes=None |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|map_dot_label=Sannōzuka Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=23|coordinates=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|12=draft}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川|12=draft}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)|12=draft}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|12=draft}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier typeing a flat plattype, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south. burial goods recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. Based on its design and burial goods, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == burial == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following area: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves burial goods from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! type !! Length !! built !! Heritage Status |- | {{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳|12=draft}} || Keyhole-shaped || 120m || Late 5th century || National Historic Site |- | [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]|| Keyhole-shaped || 123m || Early 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳|12=draft}} || Keyhole-shaped || 128m || Late 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Katatsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)|12=draft}} || Scallop-shaped || 80m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|12=draft}} || Keyhole-shaped || This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |alternate_name=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |part_of={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} |burial goods=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |notes=None |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|map_dot_label=Sannōzuka Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=23|coordinates=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|12=draft}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川|12=draft}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)|12=draft}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|12=draft}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier typeing a flat plattype, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south. burial goods recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. Based on its design and burial goods, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == burial == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following area: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves burial goods from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! type !! Length !! built !! Keyhole-shaped || 123m || Early 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳|12=draft}} || Keyhole-shaped || 128m || Late 6th century || National Historic Site |- | So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |alternate_name=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |part_of={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |notes=None |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|map_dot_label=Sannōzuka Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=23|coordinates=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川|12=draft|12=draft}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)|12=draft|12=draft}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|12=draft|12=draft}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier typeing a flat plattype, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the south. burial goods recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}. Based on its design and burial goods, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == burial == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following area: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves burial goods from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! type !! Length !! built !! Heritage Status |- | {{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|ja|摩利支天塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} || Keyhole-shaped || 120m || Late 5th century || National Historic Site |- | [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]|| Keyhole-shaped || 123m || Early 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} || Keyhole-shaped || 128m || Late 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Katatsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)|12=draft|12=draft}} || Scallop-shaped || 80m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|12=draft|12=draft}} || Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490093|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Fujita, Norio|year=2002|chapter=Sannōzuka Kofun|title={{ill|続 So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |alternate_name=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |part_of={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |burial goods=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |notes=None |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|map_dot_label=Sannōzuka Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=23|coordinates=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier typeing a flat plattype, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} opening to the south. burial goods recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Based on its design and burial goods, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == burial == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following area: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves burial goods from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! type !! Length !! built !! revision.comment: 12 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:山王塚古墳 (下野市) up to rev 100257001
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 山王塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（中央に後円部、左奥に前方部） |別名 = 国分寺山王塚古墳 |所属 = しもつけ古墳群（飯塚・国分寺地域）/国分古墳群 |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[下野市]]国分寺1404 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=山王塚古墳}} |形状 = [[前方後円墳]]（下野型古墳） |規模 = 墳丘長89m |埋葬施設 = 半地下式[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 鉄地銀張製帯先金具・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=山王塚古墳|lat_deg=36|lat_min=23|lat_sec=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|23|20|N|139|48|22|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 260| height =300 | caption = {{center|しもつけ古墳群（飯塚・国分寺地域）
分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E}} | label1=摩利支天塚 | label-pos1=right | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|22|30.88|N|139|48|25.67|E}} | label2=琵琶塚 | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|24|15.15|N|139|48|41.13|E}} | label3=吾妻 | label-pos3=right | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-南南東.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|23|1.80|N|139|48|18.15|E}} | label4=甲塚 | label-pos4=left | label-color4=black | mark4=帆立貝形古墳-南.png | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|23|33.06|N|139|48|23.26|E}} | label5=愛宕塚 | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=前方後円墳-西南西.png | mark-size5=25 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E}} | label6=山王塚 | label-pos6=left | label-color6=black | mark6=前方後円墳-西.png | mark-size6=25 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|36|23|41.67|N|139|48|39.60|E}} | label7=丸塚 | label-pos7=right | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.png | mark-size7=25 | mark-coord8= {{Coord|36|23|6.21|N|139|48|21.95|E}} | label8=下野国分寺跡 | label-pos8=top | label-color8=black | mark8=Red pog.svg | mark-size8=10 | mark-coord9= {{Coord|36|23|6.98|N|139|48|44.73|E}} | label9=下野国分尼寺跡 | label-pos9=right | label-color9=black | mark9=Red pog.svg | mark-size9=10 | mark-coord10= {{Coord|36|22|47.5|N|139|47|6.3|E}} | label10=下野国庁跡 | label-pos10=right | label-color10=black | mark10=Red pog.svg | mark-size10=10 }} '''山王塚古墳'''（さんのうづかこふん、'''国分寺山王塚古墳'''）は、[[栃木県]][[下野市]]国分寺にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。しもつけ古墳群（うち飯塚・国分寺地域）を構成する古墳の1つ。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+しもつけ古墳群（飯塚・国分寺地域）一覧 !古墳名!!形状!!規模!!築造時期!!史跡指定 |- |[[摩利支天塚古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長120m||5c末||国史跡 |- |[[琵琶塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長123m||6c初頭||国史跡 |- |[[吾妻古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長128m||6c後半||国史跡 |- |[[甲塚古墳 (下野市)|甲塚古墳]]||[[帆立貝形古墳]]||墳丘長80m||6c後半||なし |- |[[愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|愛宕塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長78.5m||6c末||県史跡 |- |'''山王塚古墳'''||前方後円墳||墳丘長89m||6c末-7c初頭||なし |- |[[丸塚古墳 (下野市)|丸塚古墳]]||[[円墳]]||直径65m||7c前半||県史跡 |}
[[ファイル:国分寺山王塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館展示。}}]] 栃木県南部、[[下野市]]西部の[[姿川]]と[[思川 (栃木県)|思川]]に挟まれた台地上に築造された古墳である。東には[[丸塚古墳 (下野市)|丸塚古墳]]、南西には[[愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|愛宕塚古墳]]が所在する。現在までに墳丘周囲は削平されているほか、[[1987年|1987]]-[[1989年]]（[[昭和]]62-[[平成]]元年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|山王塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 墳形は前方後円形で（調査以前は前方後方形とする説もあった）、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘は2段築成で、特に1段目は平坦な基壇状を呈し、下野地域特有の「下野型古墳」の特徴を示す。墳丘長は89メートルを測る{{Sfn|山王塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘外表で[[埴輪]]は認められていない。また墳丘周囲には逆盾形の周溝が巡り、周溝を含めた古墳全体としては100メートル以上におよぶ。埋葬施設は前方部のくびれ部寄りにおける半地下式[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する{{Sfn|山王塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。出土遺物としては鉄地銀張製帯先金具・[[須恵器]]甕片がある{{Sfn|山王塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期-[[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[6世紀]]末-[[7世紀]]初頭[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 山王塚古墳]（下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム）。（または6世紀末{{Sfn|山王塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}）頃と推定される。しもつけ古墳群の飯塚・国分寺地域では、前方後円墳から[[円墳]]への移行のなかで最後の前方後円墳として位置づけられる古墳になる{{Sfn|山王塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1987年|1987]]-[[1989年]]（[[昭和]]62-[[平成]]元年）、発掘調査（栃木県立しもつけ風土記の丘資料館、[[1988年|1988]]・1989・[[1990年]]に報告）。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては、前方部のくびれ部寄りにおいて半地下式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する{{Sfn|山王塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。石室の規模として、玄室は長さ6.1メートル・幅2.6メートル・推定高さ2.6メートルを測る{{Sfn|山王塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 玄室の奥壁・前壁は[[凝灰岩]]の一枚石によって構築される。特に前壁は一枚石の中央を刳り抜く「刳り抜き玄門」である{{Sfn|山王塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。側壁は川原石の小口積みで、三重の控え積みを伴う{{Sfn|山王塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 関連施設 == * しもつけ風土記の丘資料館（下野市国分寺） - 山王塚古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=国分古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|国分古墳群（平凡社）|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=藤田典夫|year=2002|chapter=山王塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|山王塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author1=小森紀男|author2=黒田理史|year=1988|chapter=国分寺町山王塚古墳第1次発掘調査報告|title=栃木県立しもつけ風土記の丘資料館年報 第2号|publisher=栃木県教育委員会|isbn=|page=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author1=小森紀男|author2=黒田理史|year=1989|chapter=国分寺町山王塚古墳第2次発掘調査報告|title=栃木県立しもつけ風土記の丘資料館年報 第3号|publisher=栃木県教育委員会|isbn=|page=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author1=小森紀男|author2=黒田理史|year=1990|chapter=国分寺町山王塚古墳第3次発掘調査報告|title=栃木県立しもつけ風土記の丘資料館年報 第4号|publisher=栃木県教育委員会|isbn=|page=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author1=小森紀男|author2=齋藤恒夫|title=大形前方後円墳の築造企画(1) -栃木県国分寺町山王塚古墳の復元をめぐって-|date=1992-03-31|journal=研究紀要|publisher=財団法人栃木県文化振興事業団埋蔵文化財センター|volume=|number=1|pages=85-106|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/105394|location=|ncid=AN10460944|doi=10.24484/sitereports.105394|ref=}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 山王塚古墳] - 下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム {{DEFAULTSORT:さんのうつかこふん しもつけし}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:下野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

[[ファイル:国分寺山王塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館展示。}}]] 栃木県南部、[[下野市]]西部の[[姿川]]と[[思川 (栃木県)|思川]]に挟まれた台地上に築造された古墳である。東には[[丸塚古墳 (下野市)|丸塚古墳]]、南西には[[愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|愛宕塚古墳]]が所在する。現在までに墳丘周囲は削平されているほか、[[1987年|1987]]-[[1989年]]（[[昭和]]62-[[平成]]元年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|山王塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 墳形は前方後円形で（調査以前は前方後方形とする説もあった）、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘は2段築成で、特に1段目は平坦な基壇状を呈し、下野地域特有の「下野型古墳」の特徴を示す。墳丘長は89メートルを測る{{Sfn|山王塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘外表で[[埴輪]]は認められていない。また墳丘周囲には逆盾形の周溝が巡り、周溝を含めた古墳全体としては100メートル以上におよぶ。埋葬施設は前方部のくびれ部寄りにおける半地下式[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する{{Sfn|山王塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。出土遺物としては鉄地銀張製帯先金具・[[須恵器]]甕片がある{{Sfn|山王塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期-[[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[6世紀]]末-[[7世紀]]初頭[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 山王塚古墳]（下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム）。（または6世紀末{{Sfn|山王塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}）頃と推定される。しもつけ古墳群の飯塚・国分寺地域では、前方後円墳から[[円墳]]への移行のなかで最後の前方後円墳として位置づけられる古墳になる{{Sfn|山王塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1987年|1987]]-[[1989年]]（[[昭和]]62-[[平成]]元年）、発掘調査（栃木県立しもつけ風土記の丘資料館、[[1988年|1988]]・1989・[[1990年]]に報告）。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては、前方部のくびれ部寄りにおいて半地下式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する{{Sfn|山王塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。石室の規模として、玄室は長さ6.1メートル・幅2.6メートル・推定高さ2.6メートルを測る{{Sfn|山王塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 玄室の奥壁・前壁は[[凝灰岩]]の一枚石によって構築される。特に前壁は一枚石の中央を刳り抜く「刳り抜き玄門」である{{Sfn|山王塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。側壁は川原石の小口積みで、三重の控え積みを伴う{{Sfn|山王塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 関連施設 == * しもつけ風土記の丘資料館（下野市国分寺） - 山王塚古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=国分古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|国分古墳群（平凡社）|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=藤田典夫|year=2002|chapter=山王塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|山王塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author1=小森紀男|author2=黒田理史|year=1988|chapter=国分寺町山王塚古墳第1次発掘調査報告|title=栃木県立しもつけ風土記の丘資料館年報 第2号|publisher=栃木県教育委員会|isbn=|page=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author1=小森紀男|author2=黒田理史|year=1989|chapter=国分寺町山王塚古墳第2次発掘調査報告|title=栃木県立しもつけ風土記の丘資料館年報 第3号|publisher=栃木県教育委員会|isbn=|page=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author1=小森紀男|author2=黒田理史|year=1990|chapter=国分寺町山王塚古墳第3次発掘調査報告|title=栃木県立しもつけ風土記の丘資料館年報 第4号|publisher=栃木県教育委員会|isbn=|page=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author1=小森紀男|author2=齋藤恒夫|title=大形前方後円墳の築造企画(1) -栃木県国分寺町山王塚古墳の復元をめぐって-|date=1992-03-31|journal=研究紀要|publisher=財団法人栃木県文化振興事業団埋蔵文化財センター|volume=|number=1|pages=85-106|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/105394|location=|ncid=AN10460944|doi=10.24484/sitereports.105394|ref=}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 山王塚古墳] - Kofun (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |alternate_name=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |part_of={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |burial goods=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |notes=None |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|map_dot_label=Sannōzuka Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=23|coordinates=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier typeing a flat plattype, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} opening to the south. burial goods recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Based on its design and burial goods, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == burial == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following area: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves burial goods from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! type !! Kofun (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |alternate_name=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |part_of={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |burial goods=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |notes=None |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|map_dot_label=Sannōzuka Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=23|coordinates=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier typeing a flat plattype, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} opening to the south. burial goods recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Based on its design and burial goods, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == burial == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following area: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves burial goods from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! type !! Kofun (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |alternate_name=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |part_of={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |burial goods=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |notes=None |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|map_dot_label=Sannōzuka Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=23|coordinates=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier typeing a flat plattype, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} opening to the south. burial goods recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Based on its design and burial goods, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == burial == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following area: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves burial goods from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! type !! Length !! built !! Keyhole-shaped || 123m || Early 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Azuma Kofun|ja|吾妻古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} || Keyhole-shaped || 128m || Late 6th century || National Historic Site |- | It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == burial == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following area: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves burial goods from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! type !! So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |alternate_name=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |part_of={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Group|qq=}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke, Tochigi|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |notes=None |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|map_dot_label=Sannōzuka Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=23|coordinates=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke, Tochigi|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Group|qq=}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata River|qq=}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omoi River (Tochigi)|qq=}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier typeing a flat plattype, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. burial goods recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}. Based on its design and burial goods, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == burial == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following area: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves burial goods from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! type !! Length !! built !! Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |alternate_name=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |part_of={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Group|qq=}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke, Tochigi|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |notes=None |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|map_dot_label=Sannōzuka Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=23|coordinates=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|ja|下野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke, Tochigi|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Group|qq=}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata River|qq=}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omoi River (Tochigi)|qq=}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|lt=Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier typeing a flat plattype, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. burial goods recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}. Based on its design and burial goods, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == burial == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following area: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves burial goods from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! type !! Length !! built !! But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |alternate_name=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |part_of={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Group|qq=}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location=[[:en:Shimotsuke, Tochigi|Shimotsuke, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods=Iron fittings with silver inlay, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |notes=None |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|map_dot_label=Sannōzuka Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=23|coordinates=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in [[:en:Shimotsuke, Tochigi|Shimotsuke, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Group|qq=}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata River|qq=}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omoi River (Tochigi)|qq=}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|lt=Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier typeing a flat plattype, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. burial goods recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. Based on its design and burial goods, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == burial == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following area: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves burial goods from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! type !! Length !! built !! Keyhole-shaped || 123m || Early 6th century || National Historic Site |- | [[:en:Azuma Kofun|Azuma Kofun]] || Keyhole-shaped || 128m || Late 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Katatsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katatsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} || Scallop-shaped || 80m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Group|qq=}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata River|qq=}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omoi River (Tochigi)|qq=}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|lt=Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier typeing a flat plattype, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. burial goods recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. Based on its design and burial goods, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == burial == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following area: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves burial goods from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! type !! Length !! built !! Keyhole-shaped || 123m || Early 6th century || National Historic Site |- | [[:en:Azuma Kofun|Azuma Kofun]] || Keyhole-shaped || 128m || Late 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Katatsuka It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier typeing a flat plattype, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. burial goods recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. Based on its design and burial goods, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == burial == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following area: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves burial goods from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! type !! Kofun (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |alternate_name=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |part_of={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location=[[:en:Shimotsuke, Tochigi|Shimotsuke, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods=Iron fittings with silver inlay, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |notes=None |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|map_dot_label=Sannōzuka Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=23|coordinates=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in [[:en:Shimotsuke, Tochigi|Shimotsuke, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata River|qq=}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omoi River (Tochigi)|qq=}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|lt=Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier typeing a flat plattype, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. burial goods recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. Based on its design and burial goods, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == burial == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following area: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves burial goods from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! type !! A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |alternate_name=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |part_of={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location=[[:en:Shimotsuke, Tochigi|Shimotsuke, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods=Iron fittings with silver inlay, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |notes=None |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|map_dot_label=Sannōzuka Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=23|coordinates=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in [[:en:Shimotsuke, Tochigi|Shimotsuke, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata River|qq=}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omoi River (Tochigi)|qq=}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|lt=Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier typeing a flat plattype, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. burial goods recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. Based on its design and burial goods, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == burial == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following area: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves burial goods from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! type !! Length !! built !! Heritage Status |- | [[:en:Marishitenzuka Kofun|Marishitenzuka Kofun]] || Keyhole-shaped || 120m || Late 5th century || National Historic Site |- | [[:en:Biwazuka Kofun|Biwazuka Kofun]]|| Keyhole-shaped || 123m || Early 6th century || National Historic Site |- | [[:en:Azuma Kofun|Azuma Kofun]] || Keyhole-shaped || 128m || Late 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Katatsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|甲塚古墳 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katatsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} || Scallop-shaped || 80m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun
