[[ja:山王塚古墳 (下野市)]] [[ja:山王塚古墳 (下野市)]] [[ja:山王塚古墳 (下野市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q123120887]] Wikidata name: Sannōzuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |alternate_name=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |part_of={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491515}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|WD=Q851804|lt=Shimotsuke, Tochigi}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |notes=None |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|map_dot_label=Sannōzuka Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=23|coordinates=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|WD=Q851804|lt=Shimotsuke, Tochigi}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491515}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata River|1=Sugata River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447636}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omoi River (Tochigi)|1=Omoi River (Tochigi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491515}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|lt=Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier typeing a flat plattype, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. burial goods recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}. Based on its design and burial goods, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == burial == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following area: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves burial goods from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! type !! Length !! built !! Heritage Status |- | {{ill|Marishitenzuka Kofun|en|Marishitenzuka Kofun|WD=Q17210244|lt=Marishitenzuka Kofun}} || Keyhole-shaped || 120m || Late 5th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Biwazuka Kofun|en|Biwazuka Kofun|WD=Q17230002|lt=Biwazuka Kofun}}|| Keyhole-shaped || 123m || Early 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Azuma Kofun|en|Azuma (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} || Scallop-shaped || 80m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} || Keyhole-shaped || 78.5m || Late 6th century || Prefectural Historic Site |- | '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || 89m || It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491515}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata River|1=Sugata River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447636}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omoi River (Tochigi)|1=Omoi River (Tochigi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491515}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|lt=Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier typeing a flat plattype, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. burial goods recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}. Based on its design and burial goods, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == burial == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following area: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves burial goods from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! type !! Length !! built !! {{ill|Biwazuka Kofun|en|Biwazuka Kofun|WD=Q17230002|lt=Biwazuka Kofun}}|| Keyhole-shaped || 123m || Early 6th century || National Historic Site |- | {{ill|Azuma Kofun|en|Azuma (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} || Scallop-shaped || 80m || Late 6th century || None |- | {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} || Keyhole-shaped || 78.5m || Late 6th century || Prefectural Historic Site |- | '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' || Keyhole-shaped || 89m || of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. burial goods recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}. Based on its design and burial goods, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == burial == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. burial goods recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}. Based on its design and burial goods, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == burial == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following area: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves burial goods from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! type !! [[ja:山王塚古墳 (下野市)]] [[ja:山王塚古墳 (下野市)]] [[ja:山王塚古墳 (下野市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Sannōzuka Kofun |image=[[File:Sannozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (central: posterior circular mound; back left: anterior rectangular mound) |alternate_name=Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun |part_of={{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491515|1=Shimotsuke Kofun Group}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region)/Kokubun Kofun Group |location={{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|WD=Q851804|lt=Shimotsuke, Tochigi|1=Shimotsuke, Tochigi}}, Tochigi Prefecture |coordinates={{coord|36|23|38.85|N|139|48|34.73|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sannōzuka Kofun}} |type=Keyhole-shaped kofun (Shimotsuke type) |length=89m |burial=Semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods=Iron fittings with silver inlay, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} built=Late 6th century–early 7th century |notes=None |map={{location map|Japan Tochigi|map_dot_label=Sannōzuka Kofun|coordinates=36|coordinates=23|coordinates=38.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=48|lon_sec=34.73|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sannōzuka Kofun''' (山王塚古墳, also known as Kokubunji Sannōzuka Kofun) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun located in {{ill|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuke, Tochigi|WD=Q851804|lt=Shimotsuke, Tochigi|1=Shimotsuke, Tochigi}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja|しもつけ古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491515|1=Shimotsuke Kofun Group}} (Iizuka-Kokubunji region) but has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == Located on a plateau between the {{ill|Sugata River|ja|姿川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugata River|1=Sugata River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447636}} and {{ill|Omoi River (Tochigi)|ja|思川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omoi River (Tochigi)|1=Q11491515|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491515}}, Sannōzuka Kofun lies in southern Tochigi Prefecture, west of Shimotsuke City. Nearby tumuli include {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|lt=Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|丸塚古墳 (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marutsuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|ja|愛宕塚古墳 (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagozuka Kofun (Shimotsuke)|qq=}} to the southwest. The surrounding mound area has been flattened, and excavations conducted between 1987 and 1989 uncovered significant findings. The kofun is oriented with its anterior rectangular mound facing west. It is constructed in two tiers, with the first tier typeing a flat plattype, a characteristic of Shimotsuke-type kofun. The mound is 89 meters in length. A reverse shield-shaped moat encircles the mound, extending the total site length to over 100 meters. The burial facility, located near the constriction of the anterior mound, is a semi-underground {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. burial goods recovered include an iron fitting with silver inlay and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}. Based on its design and burial goods, the mound is dated to the late 6th century to early 7th century. It represents the final keyhole-shaped kofun in the transition to circular mounds in the region[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=38 Sannōzuka Kofun] (Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum).. == burial == The burial chamber is a semi-underground yokoana-style stone chamber located near the constriction of the anterior mound. It opens to the south and has the following area: * Chamber length: 6.1 meters * Chamber width: 2.6 meters * Estimated chamber height: 2.6 meters The rear and front walls of the chamber are constructed using single slabs of tuff, with the front wall featuring a unique "carved portal" design. The side walls are composed of river stones laid in courses with triple reinforcements. == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum (Shimotsuke City) – Displays and preserves burial goods from Sannōzuka Kofun. == Timeline == * 1987–1989: Excavation surveys conducted by the Tochigi Prefecture Shimotsuke Fudoki Hill Museum. == Related Sites in Shimotsuke Kofun Group (Iizuka-Kokubunji Region) == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ ! name !! type !!
